description: BOM handling and parser robustness: read STF files using utf-8-sig to handle Byte Order Marks and update the writer/reader to normalise CRLF lines consistently.
steps: In src/stx/stf/parser.py parse_stf(), change encoding from 'utf-8' to 'utf-8-sig' so that a leading BOM (\ufeff) is automatically stripped
In parse_stf_text(), add a line at the start to strip a leading BOM if present: text = text.lstrip('\ufeff')

In parse_stf_text() or _parse_lines(), normalise CRLF to LF before splitting: text = text.replace('
', '
').replace('', '
') before calling splitlines() -- actually splitlines() already handles this, but ensure the raw_line values don't contain trailing

In src/stx/stf/writer.py _write_lf_utf8(), the existing CR scrubbing is already in place (data.replace(b'
', b'
').replace(b'', b'
')) - confirm this is correct
Add a test in tests/ that parses an STF file with a BOM prefix and verifies it produces the same Document as one without
Add a test that parses an STF file with CRLF line endings and verifies correct parsing
acceptance_criteria: parse_stf on a file with UTF-8 BOM (\xef\xbb\xbf prefix) succeeds and produces correct metadata
parse_stf_text on a string with BOM succeeds
CRLF line endings are handled correctly (no trailing \r in labels)
Writer output remains LF-only, no BOM
findings: All 74 tests pass. Parser now uses utf-8-sig for file reads, strips BOM in parse_stf_text, and normalises stray CR in _parse_lines. Writer was already correct. Three new tests added.
